tex:
(a) The line $XY$ touches the circle at the point $R$.
\newline

Write down the mathematical name for the line $XY$.
\newline

(b) Points $P$ and $Q$ lie on the circle.
\newline

Write down the mathematical name for the line $PQ$.
\newline

(c) The area of the circle is $43.5\,\text{cm}^2$.
\newline

Calculate the radius of the circle.
\newline

(d) The diameter of a different circle is $6.4\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Calculate the circumference of this circle.
\newline

Give your answer in millimetres.
\newline

content.content:
The line $XY$ touches the circle at the point $R$.
\newline

Write down the mathematical name for the line $XY$.
\newline

Points $P$ and $Q$ lie on the circle.
\newline

Write down the mathematical name for the line $PQ$.
\newline

The area of the circle is $43.5\,\text{cm}^2$.
\newline

Calculate the radius of the circle.
\newline

The diameter of a different circle is $6.4\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Calculate the circumference of this circle.
\newline

Give your answer in millimetres.
\newline

_rawBody:
\relinput{Figures/Q11-CircleTangent.tex}

\begin{parts}
\item The line $XY$ touches the circle at the point $R$.

\vgap

Write down the mathematical name for the line $XY$.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item Points $P$ and $Q$ lie on the circle.

\vgap

Write down the mathematical name for the line $PQ$.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item The area of the circle is 43.5\,cm$^2$.

\vgap

Calculate the radius of the circle.

\AnswerLine[][cm]
\Marks{2}

\item The diameter of a different circle is 6.4\,cm.

\vgap

Calculate the circumference of this circle.

Give your answer in millimetres.

\AnswerLine[][mm]
\Marks{3}

\end{parts}

qtype: interpreting travel graph, speed-distance-time calculations, time duration
tex:
Jason leaves Town A at 09$\,$00 and cycles to Town C.
\newline

The travel graph shows Jason's journey.
\newline

(a) Find Jason's average speed, in kilometres per hour, from Town A to Town B.
\newline

(b) Jason leaves Town C at 12$\,$00.
\newline

Jason continues to Town D at a constant speed of 15 kilometres per hour.
\newline

(i) (c) Calculate the time Jason takes to travel from Town C to Town D.
\newline

Give your answer in hours and minutes.
\newline

  h  min

(d) On the travel graph, complete Jason's journey.
\newline

(e) Find the total time, in minutes, that Jason stopped between Town A and Town D.
\newline

(f) Calculate Jason's overall average speed, in kilometres per hour, from Town A to Town D.
\newline

(g) Lisa leaves Town C at 11$\,$00 and arrives at Town A at 13$\,$42.
\newline

Lisa cycles at a constant speed on the same road as Jason, without stopping.
\newline

(i) (h) Draw a line on the travel graph to show Lisa's journey.
\newline

(i) Find the distance from Town A when Lisa and Jason pass each other.
\newline

content.content:
Find Jason's average speed, in kilometres per hour, from Town A to Town B.
\newline

Jason leaves Town C at 12$\,$00.
\newline

Jason continues to Town D at a constant speed of 15 kilometres per hour.
\newline

\newline

Give your answer in hours and minutes.
\newline

On the travel graph, complete Jason's journey.
\newline

Find the total time, in minutes, that Jason stopped between Town A and Town D.
\newline

Calculate Jason's overall average speed, in kilometres per hour, from Town A to Town D.
\newline

qtype: ratio and proportion, percentage calculations, compound interest
tex:
Jo and Mira buy a shop.
\newline

(a) They pay for the shop in the ratio Jo$\,{:}\,$Mira = 7$\,{:}\,$15.
\newline

Mira pays \$$84\,000$ more than Jo.
\newline

Work out how much they each pay.
\newline

(b) The shop makes a profit of \$$56\,000$.
\newline

Jo receives 12\% of the profit.
\newline

Mira receives \$$14\,000$ of the profit.
\newline

The rest of the profit is put into a bank account.
\newline

(i) (c) Calculate how much money Jo receives.
\newline

(d) Calculate the amount put into the bank account as a percentage of the profit.
\newline

(e) Mira invests \$$14\,000$ at a rate of 2.4\% per year compound interest.
\newline

Calculate the value of this investment at the end of 4 years.
\newline

content.content:
They pay for the shop in the ratio Jo$\,{:}\,$Mira = 7$\,{:}\,$15.
\newline

Mira pays \$$84\,000$ more than Jo.
\newline

Work out how much they each pay.
\newline

The shop makes a profit of \$$56\,000$.
\newline

Jo receives 12\% of the profit.
\newline

Mira receives \$$14\,000$ of the profit.
\newline

The rest of the profit is put into a bank account.
\newline

Calculate how much money Jo receives.
\newline

Calculate the amount put into the bank account as a percentage of the profit.
\newline

Mira invests \$$14\,000$ at a rate of 2.4\% per year compound interest.
\newline

Calculate the value of this investment at the end of 4 years.
\newline

stem.content:
Jo and Mira buy a shop.
\newline

_rawBody:
Jo and Mira buy a shop.

\begin{parts}
\item They pay for the shop in the ratio Jo\,:\,Mira = 7\,:\,15.

Mira pays \$84\,000 more than Jo.

\vgap

Work out how much they each pay.

\AnswerLine[Jo \$ = ][]

\vgap

\AnswerLine[Mira \$ = ][]
\Marks{3}

\item The shop makes a profit of \$56\,000.

Jo receives 12\% of the profit.

Mira receives \$14\,000 of the profit.

The rest of the profit is put into a bank account.

\begin{subparts}
\item Calculate how much money Jo receives.

\AnswerLine[\$ ][]
\Marks{1}

\item Calculate the amount put into the bank account as a percentage of the profit.

\AnswerLine[][\%]
\Marks{2}

\item Mira invests \$14\,000 at a rate of 2.4\% per year compound interest.

\vgap

Calculate the value of this investment at the end of 4 years.

\AnswerLine[\$ ][]
\Marks{2}

\end{subparts}

\end{parts}

qtype: finding terms in an arithmetic sequence, finding nth term, checking if a number is a term
tex:
(a) These are the first five terms of a sequence.
\newline

$$7   a   b   c   31$$

In the sequence, the same number is added each time to obtain the next term.
\newline

Find the value of each of the terms $a$, $b$ and $c$.
\newline

(b) These are the first five terms of another sequence.
\newline

$$4   11   18   25   32$$

(i) (c) Find the $n$th term of the sequence.
\newline

(d) Show that 361 is a term in the sequence.
\newline

content.content:
These are the first five terms of a sequence.
\newline

$$7 \quad a \quad b \quad c \quad 31$$

In the sequence, the same number is added each time to obtain the next term.
\newline

Find the value of each of the terms $a$, $b$ and $c$.
\newline

These are the first five terms of another sequence.
\newline

$$4 \quad 11 \quad 18 \quad 25 \quad 32$$

Find the $n$th term of the sequence.
\newline

Show that 361 is a term in the sequence.
\newline
